title: Chewy Southern Thai Padron Pepper Curry
description: A briny and chewy one-pot Thai dish featuring padron peppers, this southern Thai curry is pescatarian-friendly and cooked in a pandan leaf wrap, reducing the need for multiple vessels.
instructions: Cut the pandan leaves into 12 inch squares and set aside for wrapping the curry.
Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the curry paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, palm sugar, and salt, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the padron peppers and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add the shrimp and cook, stirring occasionally, until the shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Wrap the curry in the pandan leaves and serve hot.
